subject: Political Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the concept of gender division in society
Recognize the role of feminist movements in promoting equality
Understand the meaning of communalism and its impact on democracy
Explain the concept of secularism in the Indian Constitution
Understand caste inequalities and their historical roots
Analyse the relationship between caste and politics
Develop awareness of social diversity and equality in a democratic society

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Social Divisions
topics_covered: Gender, religion, and caste as social differences
teaching_strategy: Use real-life examples of social diversity
student_activities: Students list examples of social differences in society
Group discussion on diversity in their surroundings

lesson_plan.title: Gender Division in Society
teaching_strategy: Discussion on roles of men and women in families
student_activities: Observe household work distribution at home
Write short notes on gender roles

lesson_plan.title: Feminist Movements and Women's Participation
teaching_strategy: Discussion on equality in education and jobs
student_activities: Share examples of women in different professions

lesson_plan.title: Religion and Politics
teaching_strategy: Encourage discussion on harmony among religions

lesson_plan.title: Secularism and the Constitution
student_activities: Write two constitutional provisions related to secularism

lesson_plan.title: Caste Inequalities in Society
teaching_strategy: Discuss contributions of Ambedkar, Phule, and Periyar
student_activities: Prepare short notes on social reformers

lesson_plan.title: Caste and Politics
topics_covered: Role of caste in electoral politics
Positive and negative aspects of caste politics

teacher_tip: Use real-life examples and class discussions to help students understand how gender, religion, and caste influence society and politics in India.
